Notice Of Motion And Motion To Comepl Defendant Gelmer Dubon'S Written Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-19-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

3 - PLAINTIFF CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To Comepl Defendant Gelmer Dubon'S Written Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions.

Plaintiff CSAA Insurance Exchange's Motion To Compel To Compel Defendant Gelmer Dubon's Written Discovery Responses And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART.
-
Good cause appearing and pursuant to the California Discovery Act, no later than 15 days after notice of this order, Defendant Gelmer Dubon must provide verified, objection free responses to the following discovery served by Plaintiff CSAA Insurance Exchange on November 26, 2024: (1) Form Interrogatories, Set One and (2) Requests for Production, Set One to Dubon.

The record does not show Defendant Gelmer Dubon acted with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant Gelmer Dubon shall pay $1,650 to Plaintiff as sanctions, payment no later than June 13, 2025.

DEFENDANT IS ADVISED stacked discovery motions are strongly disfavored. Going forward, stacked motions will be ordered off calendar in favor of individual motions focused each on a single discovery vehicle (e.g., Frogs, SRogs, RFPs, RFAs, deposition notice) addressed to a single, specific party.

Order is signed

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)
